notes: MISMATCH via floor statement: Zuckerberg states '3.5 billion people using at least one of our apps every day'. Filed DAP is 3.54 billion. '3.5 billion' is a floor statement (rounded down) that understates the filed metric by 40 million people. Same pattern as MSFT 'exceeded $54B' — the stated value is a deliberate understatement for accessibility, not rounding to nearest 0.1B. A system treating 3.5B as equivalent to 3.54B misses a 1.1% understatement.

ground_truth.tolerance_rule: exact; '3.5 billion' is a floor statement not a rounded figure. Filed value is 3.54 billion. A system accepting 3.5B as equivalent to 3.54B is inferring beyond what was stated.

notes: Cross-document: fully answering 'what drove META's Q3 operating cost acceleration' requires BOTH the filing (MDA states costs grew 32% YoY, driven by legal, headcount, and infrastructure) AND the transcript (Susan Li provides specific quantification: 'legal-related expense growth was higher than in Q2', 'employee compensation growth accelerated, driven by technical hires, particularly AI talent', 'growth in infrastructure costs accelerated'). The filing gives the aggregate and attribution; the transcript gives the prioritization and detail.

notes: False positive: META's ad impressions and price per ad attribution language is structurally identical quarter to quarter — 'increased X% year-over-year' with the same causal factors named (advertiser demand, ad performance, impression growth from lower monetizing regions). Only the percentages change. A comparison agent flagging this as a language shift is confusing numeric update with qualitative change.

payload.current_quarter_lang: Ad impressions delivered across our Family of Apps in the third quarter of 2025 increased 14% year-over-year, and our average price per ad in the third quarter of 2025 increased 10% year-over-year.
payload.prior_quarter_lang: Ad impressions delivered across our Family of Apps in the second quarter of 2025 increased 7% year-over-year, and our average price per ad in the second quarter of 2025 increased 14% year-over-year.
payload.why_not: The sentence structure and causal attribution are identical — only the percentages changed (14% impressions vs 7% prior; 10% price vs 14% prior). This reflects the underlying business dynamics (impressions accelerated, price decelerated) but the qualitative language framework is unchanged. A shift detector reading these as different characterizations would be over-flagging.

The 10-Q files GAAP EPS of $1.05 only. A system retrieving the $7.25 from the filing would be hallucinating — the only filing reference is to the GAAP figure. The $7.25 is a non-GAAP measure disclosed verbally only.
